title: cerastium decalvans f. dollineri
intro: Cerastium decalvans f. dollineri, also known as a subspecies of the common mouse-ear chickweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cerastium decalvans f. dollineri include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, which is often used to treat colds, sore throats, and digestive issues.
